title: proofbundle trust policy v0.1
description: A relying party's machine-readable, fail-closed trust decision, applied OVER a completed crypto verification. snake_case, offline, versioned. Unknown fields are a parse error (additionalProperties: false) so a typo silently weakening a policy is impossible.

schema.description: Policy format version. v0.2 adds the additive decision_receipt section (for the decision-receipt/v0.1 predicate); v0.1 policies stay valid unchanged. The decision_receipt section is accepted ONLY under v0.2 (the parser fails closed on a decision_receipt section declared under v0.1).

policy_id.description: A stable human-readable identifier for this policy, echoed in verify output so a logged decision names the policy it applied.

deploymentReady.description: AP-2 §6.2 template metadata. false = a raw TEMPLATE, not a deployment-ready policy (it pins no signer identity); `policy lint --strict` fails on it and verify can never yield safeForAutomation:true. Set true only by `policy instantiate` once every required field is pinned. Absent on a hand-written production policy.

requiresIdentityOverlay.description: AP-2 §6.2 template metadata. true = this profile is a template that MUST be completed with a signer-identity overlay (allowed_issuers / trusted_decision_makers) before use; while true, verify never sets signer_trusted (so never safeForAutomation:true). `policy instantiate` sets it false.

valid_until.description: AP-2 §6.4 lifecycle expiry (ISO-8601 UTC, e.g. 2027-01-01T00:00:00Z). Optional; since 3.1.3 ENFORCED by the policy evaluation on both paths (POLICY: FAIL, exit 3), and `policy lint` fails once it is in the past. null/absent = no expiry.

valid_from.description: A-P0-2 lifecycle not-before (ISO-8601 UTC). Optional; a policy whose valid_from is still in the future FAILS evaluation on both paths (POLICY: FAIL, exit 3). null/absent = no lower bound.

policyPurpose.description: A-P0-4 purpose binding: which verifier path this policy is for. The eval verify path accepts only 'eval', the decision path only 'decision'; 'outcome'/'trust-pack'/'public-transparency' are reserved for the 3.2.0 verifiers. Absent = transitional legacy default; strict lint requires the field.

generatedFromTemplate.description: A-P0-5 template provenance, stamped by `policy instantiate` (the canonical template name). Display/audit only; reserved — an instantiate overlay can never set it.

allowed_schema_versions.description: Bundle schema versions this policy accepts (e.g. ["proofbundle/v0.1"]). Empty/absent = do not constrain the schema version.

allowed_issuers.description: The set of signing keys the relying party trusts. The signer is matched by PUBLIC KEY (kid is a display hint only). Empty/absent = do not constrain the issuer (verify still proves internal consistency, but attributes to nobody in particular).

issuer.description: Human-readable label for logs; NOT used for matching.

public_key_b64.description: Base64 raw Ed25519 public key the signer must match.

kid.description: Optional key id hint; display only.

allowed_algs.description: Signature algorithms this policy accepts (e.g. ["ed25519"]). Empty/absent = do not constrain the alg.

require_expected_signer.description: If true, the bundle signer MUST appear in allowed_issuers (fail if allowed_issuers is empty). Default false.

required_hash_alg.description: If set, merkle.hash_alg MUST equal this (e.g. "sha256-rfc6962").

require_authenticated_root.description: If true, the stated Merkle root MUST be authenticated — it must match a relying-party --expected-root or a trusted_roots entry — else POLICY FAIL (ADR 0004, coherent-rewrap guard).

trusted_roots.description: Base64 Merkle roots the relying party trusts out of band (each MUST decode to exactly 32 bytes — hard-validated at load since 3.1.3, A-P0-5); a stated root among them counts as ROOT-BYTES authenticated (never tree context). A non-empty list enforces on its own.

trusted_checkpoints.description: A-P0-1: signed checkpoints pinning (origin, treeSize, root) ATOMICALLY. Only a bundle whose root AND tree_size both match one authenticated (signature-valid, unexpired, supported-alg) entry reaches TREE_CONTEXT_AUTHENTICITY: PASS. A non-empty list enforces on its own.

origin.description: The C2SP checkpoint origin (schemeless log identity, no whitespace, no '+').

root.description: Standard base64 of the 32-byte Merkle root at treeSize.

treeSize.description: The tree size the root is stated at — authenticated TOGETHER with the root.

hashAlg.description: Tree hashing algorithm; this format version authenticates sha256-rfc6962 only (anything else fails closed).

checkpointSigner.description: The log's C2SP verifier key (name+hexKeyID+base64(0x01‖pubkey), SPEC §7c).

validUntil.description: ISO-8601; an expired checkpoint never authenticates (fail-closed).

signature.description: Base64 of the checkpoint signature line blob: keyID[4] ‖ Ed25519 signature[64] over the reconstructed note text.

require_key_binding_when_cnf_present.description: If true, an SD-JWT that bound a cnf holder key MUST present a valid Key Binding JWT (the verifier already fails closed on this; the policy makes the requirement explicit). Default false.

expected_aud.description: If set, the KB-JWT aud MUST equal this (RFC 9901 audience binding). null = do not constrain.

require_nonce.description: If true, the presentation MUST carry a KB-JWT nonce (replay binding). Default false.

max_iat_age_seconds.description: If set, the eval claim's signed timestamp MUST be at most this many seconds old at verify time. null = do not judge freshness.

expected_vct.description: Finding 20 / issue #27 (2026-07-15). If set, the SD-JWT's disclosed vct (verifiable credential type) MUST equal this exact string, matched as an opaque identifier and NEVER dereferenced even if it looks like a URL (SSRF-safe by construction). Read ONLY from a VERIFIED issuer signature (sd-jwt-issuer-signature) — an unverified vct proves nothing and fails closed. null = do not constrain.

status.description: Revocation policy. NOTE: verify --policy v0.1 has no status-snapshot input, so a policy that ENABLES a status requirement (reject_self_issued=true or a non-empty allowed_status_authorities) fails closed with a clear reason — evaluate status separately with verify_status_snapshot. The section is accepted so a policy can declare its intent forward-compatibly.

reject_self_issued.description: If true, a status list signed by the receipt's own key is not acceptable revocation evidence.

allowed_status_authorities.description: Base64 public keys of accepted status authorities.

minimum_level.description: If set, the eval claim's assurance_level MUST be at least this (ordered weakest→strongest). null = do not constrain.

reject_self_attested_without_prereg.description: If true, a self_attested claim with no prereg_sha256 (the weakest, publish-best-of-many combination) is rejected. Default false.

decision_receipt.description: v0.2-only. Trust rules for the decision-receipt/v0.1 predicate. Accepted ONLY when schema == proofbundle/trust-policy/v0.2 (the parser fails closed otherwise). The signer is matched to trusted_decision_makers by PUBLIC KEY; decisionMaker.id is only a hint that must not conflict.

trusted_decision_makers.description: Signing keys the relying party trusts as decision makers. Matched by public_key_b64.

id.description: decisionMaker.id this key is trusted for (hint; must not conflict).

kid.description: Optional key id hint; display only.

accepted_predicate_types.description: Allowed predicateType URIs (confusion defense: a decision receipt presented as another predicate fails).

required_evidence_relations.description: evidenceRefs[].relation values that MUST be present.

require_policy_digest.description: If true, policyBoundary.policyDigest MUST be present.

require_external_anchor.description: If true, the receipt MUST carry an external anchor (see allow_pending).

allow_pending.description: If false (default), a pending anchor does NOT satisfy require_external_anchor.

anchors.description: v0.2-only (WP-A1). The relying party's anchor requirement as a policy key, accepted ONLY when schema == proofbundle/trust-policy/v0.2 (fail-closed otherwise). require_anchor: 'any' or a specific anchor type; require_anchor_target pins WHICH target the verifying anchor must stamp — matching the type alone lets a receipt anchor stamped today satisfy a relying party who meant backdating protection (preRegistration). A CLI flag that conflicts with these values is an ambiguity error (exit 2), never a silent override; allow_pending mirrors --allow-pending.

relations.description: v0.2-only (relation/v0.1 lineage profile, EXPERIMENTAL 3.3.0). require_relation_resolution: named relations that, when they appear as edges, MUST resolve (target attached and standalone-verified) — a DECLARED_UNRESOLVED edge of a named relation fails the policy (LINEAGE_REQUIREMENT_FAILED, exit 3). reject_superseded: an attached, verified receipt that declares a successor relation over the receipt under verification blocks automation. Enforced by the decision verify path; never changes the crypto verdict.
